pattern: Making every cell equal by repeatedly adding or subtracting a fixed step is only possible when every cell shares the same remainder mod that step, and once possible the median minimizes the total steps.
hints: Check whether the grid can ever become uniform at all before trying to minimize anything: every value must leave the same remainder when divided by the step.
If it is possible, the target value that minimizes total moves is always one of the existing cell values — specifically the median.
Flatten the grid, verify every value shares one remainder mod x (else return -1), sort the flattened values, pick the middle one as the target, and sum abs(value - target) / x over all cells.

edge_inputs.description: impossible because remainders mod x differ
already uniform grid needs zero operations
x equal to one, every difference counts directly

stress.description: random grid built from a common base and step so it is always feasible
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    m = rng.randint(1, min(50, total))
    n = max(1, total // m)
    x = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    base = rng.randint(1, 100)
    grid = [[base + x * rng.randint(0, 20) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid, x]
